title: Connect and Operate MCP in Codex and Claude · Protocol Clinic: Client Differences
summary: Configure one safe server in both Codex and Claude Code. Planned artifact: Add and verify the agent ext client differences increment in cross-client MCP configuration and smoke tests. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Document client-specific config, approval, prompt, resource, and output behavior.
Verify initialization, primitive discovery, tool calls, errors, and shutdown in each client.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Local MCP protocol and tool-contract portfolio."""

steps.title: Predict Client Differences
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: agent ext clients. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: agent ext clients. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Client Differences contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for document client-specific config, approval, prompt, resource, and output behavior.

content.text: Document client-specific config, approval, prompt, resource, and output behavior. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: agent ext clients. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Client Differences, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cross-client MCP configuration and smoke tests.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every MCP client exposes identical features. This fails the Client Differences contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Client Differences evidence
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-client-differences-ejri0u: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Client Differences into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-client-differences-ejri0u:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Defend the Client Differences model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Client Differences applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document client-specific config, approval, prompt, resource, and output behavior.
